description_long: This is a rectangular, two-story brick school building with a symmetrical facade. The front features a central entrance with a set of wide steps leading up to double doors, flanked by large windows arranged in a grid pattern. Above the entrance, there is a small gabled pediment with a circular clock or decorative element, adding a classical touch. The roof is flat and covered with dark brown shingles, and the building is bordered by light-colored trim. The sides and rear of the building are simpler, with rows of evenly spaced windows and no additional ornamentation. The overall appearance is solid, institutional, and functional, typical of early to mid-20th-century educational architecture.
description: A rectangular, two-story brick school building with a central entrance, symmetrical windows, and a flat, shingled roof. The facade features a gabled pediment and wide steps leading to the main doors.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance with wide steps, double doors, a gabled pediment, and a symmetrical arrangement of windows.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the long brick wall, flat roof, and a small protruding section near the front; no windows or doors visible.
description_view_2: Rear view with rows of evenly spaced windows and a plain brick wall, lacking the decorative elements of the front.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the brick wall and roof, with a small protruding section near the front.
